content.text: How can I program my application to retrieve all configuration data entered by a user across several different input fields in the UI?

This method is designed to collect every input value from the associated UI layer and store it within the instance attributes for later processing.

```python
def data(self):
        """
        This function gets back data that the user typed.
        """
        self.batch_name_value = self.ui.batch_name_value.text()
        self.saa_values = self.ui.saa_values.text()
        self.sza_values = self.ui.sza_values.text()
        self.p_values = self.ui.p_values.text()
        self.x_value = self.ui.x_value.text()
        self.y_value = self.ui.y_value.text()
        self.g_value = self.ui.g_value.text()
        self.s_value = self.ui.s_value.text()
        self.z_value = self.ui.z_value.text()
        self.wavelength_values = self.ui.wavelength_values.text()
        self.verbose_value = self.ui.verbose_value.text()
        self.phytoplankton_path = self.ui.phyto_path.text()
        self.bottom_path = self.ui.bottom_path.text()
        self.executive_path = self.ui.exec_path.text()
        self.nb_cpu = self.ui.nb_cpu.currentText()
        self.report_parameter_value = str(self.ui.report_parameter_value.text())
```

The function relies heavily on accessing string values directly from widgets; developers should ensure these retrieved strings are properly validated or type-cast before use.
What if a user leaves one of those input fields blank? Will the code handle that gracefully?
If no text is entered, methods like `.text()` or `.currentText()` will typically return an empty string (`''`). You may want to add checks for `if value:` before processing.
That makes sense, thanks for clarifying how input data is collected.
